nodes.label: Akira Kurosawa
nodes.summary: Japanese filmmaker (1910–1998) whose samurai epics reshaped world cinema.

nodes.label: The Hidden Fortress
nodes.summary: Akira Kurosawa's 1958 jidaigeki adventure film.

nodes.label: Star Wars (film)
nodes.summary: George Lucas's 1977 space-opera film.

nodes.label: George Lucas
nodes.summary: American filmmaker (b. 1944), creator of Star Wars.

nodes.label: Joseph Campbell
nodes.summary: American mythologist (1904–1987) who formulated the 'hero's journey' monomyth.

nodes.label: The Beatles
nodes.summary: English rock band (1960–1970).

nodes.label: Ravi Shankar
nodes.summary: Indian sitar virtuoso (1920–2012) who brought Hindustani classical music to the West.

nodes.label: Indian classical music
nodes.summary: The art-music tradition of the Indian subcontinent, with roots in Vedic chant.

nodes.label: blues
nodes.summary: African-American vocal and instrumental music that arose in the Deep South around 1900.

nodes.label: jazz
nodes.summary: African-American music that emerged in New Orleans in the early 20th century.

nodes.label: rock and roll
nodes.summary: Popular music that emerged in the United States in the early 1950s.

nodes.label: Alan Turing
nodes.summary: English mathematician (1912–1954), founder of theoretical computer science.

nodes.label: computer
nodes.summary: A programmable machine that carries out sequences of arithmetic or logical operations.

nodes.label: transistor
nodes.summary: The semiconductor switching device (invented 1947) that underpins all modern electronics.

nodes.label: civil rights movement
nodes.summary: The 1954–1968 U.S. non-violent movement for African-American civil rights.

nodes.label: Martin Luther King Jr.
nodes.summary: American civil-rights leader (1929–1968).

nodes.label: Mahatma Gandhi
nodes.summary: Leader of India's non-violent independence movement (1869–1948).

nodes.label: Seven Samurai
nodes.summary: Akira Kurosawa's 1954 epic, among the most influential films ever made.

nodes.label: Rashomon
nodes.summary: Akira Kurosawa's 1950 film, source of the phrase 'the Rashomon effect'.

nodes.label: The Magnificent Seven
nodes.summary: The 1960 American Western remake of Kurosawa's Seven Samurai.

nodes.label: gospel music
nodes.summary: African-American Christian devotional music that shaped soul and the civil-rights freedom songs.

nodes.label: Soul music
nodes.summary: African-American popular music that grew out of gospel and rhythm and blues.

nodes.label: funk
nodes.summary: Rhythm-driven African-American music that emerged from soul and jazz in the mid-1960s.

nodes.label: Hip hop music
nodes.summary: Music built from looped breakbeats and rhyming speech, originating in the 1970s Bronx.

nodes.label: Bob Dylan
nodes.summary: American singer-songwriter (b. 1941) who brought literary lyricism to popular music.

nodes.label: Internet
nodes.summary: The global network of interconnected computers, grown from 1960s packet-switching research.

nodes.label: television
nodes.summary: The mass broadcast medium for moving images, dominant from the mid-20th century.

nodes.label: Sputnik 1
nodes.summary: The first artificial Earth satellite, launched by the USSR in 1957.

nodes.label: Yuri Gagarin
nodes.summary: Soviet cosmonaut (1934–1968); the first human in space, 1961.

nodes.label: Tetris
nodes.summary: The 1984 tile-matching puzzle game created by Alexey Pajitnov in the USSR.

nodes.label: Apollo 11
nodes.summary: The 1969 US mission that first landed humans on the Moon.

nodes.label: Elvis Presley
nodes.summary: American singer (1935–1977), the defining star of early rock and roll.

nodes.label: Art Deco
nodes.summary: The geometric, glamorous design style of the 1920s–30s.

nodes.label: Constructivism (art)
nodes.summary: The Soviet avant-garde art and architecture movement.

nodes.label: Bauhaus
nodes.summary: The German school that fused art, craft and technology into modern design.

nodes.label: Ludwig Mies van der Rohe
nodes.summary: German-American architect, a founder of the glass-and-steel modern skyscraper.

nodes.label: International Style
nodes.summary: The dominant modernist architecture of the 20th century.

nodes.label: Le Corbusier
nodes.summary: Swiss-French architect, a pioneer of modern architecture.

nodes.label: Frank Lloyd Wright
nodes.summary: American architect of organic architecture; openly influenced by Japan.

nodes.label: Brutalist architecture
nodes.summary: The raw-concrete architecture of the postwar decades.

nodes.label: Chandigarh
nodes.summary: The Indian city planned by Le Corbusier.

nodes.label: Sydney Opera House
nodes.summary: The expressionist-modernist Australian landmark.

nodes.label: Cinema of the United States
nodes.summary: The American film industry.

nodes.label: Walt Disney
nodes.summary: American animator; pioneer of the animated feature.

nodes.label: Alfred Hitchcock
nodes.summary: British-American 'master of suspense'.

nodes.label: Citizen Kane
nodes.summary: Orson Welles's 1941 film, often called the greatest ever made.

nodes.label: Charlie Chaplin
nodes.summary: English comic actor and filmmaker of the silent era.

nodes.label: Sergio Leone
nodes.summary: Italian director who created the Spaghetti Western.

nodes.label: French New Wave
nodes.summary: The 1960s French film movement (Godard, Truffaut).

nodes.label: Italian neorealism
nodes.summary: Postwar Italian cinema shot among the poor on real streets.

nodes.label: Satyajit Ray
nodes.summary: Indian director of the Apu Trilogy; a giant of world cinema.

nodes.label: Bollywood
nodes.summary: The Hindi-language film industry of Mumbai.

nodes.label: anime
nodes.summary: Japanese animation.

nodes.label: Osamu Tezuka
nodes.summary: The 'god of manga', father of the modern anime style.

nodes.label: Steven Spielberg
nodes.summary: American director of the modern blockbuster.

nodes.label: Stanley Kubrick
nodes.summary: American director (2001: A Space Odyssey).

nodes.label: Louis Armstrong
nodes.summary: The trumpeter who made jazz a soloist's art.

nodes.label: Miles Davis
nodes.summary: Trumpeter who reinvented jazz across five decades.

nodes.label: Chuck Berry
nodes.summary: A founding father of rock and roll.

nodes.label: The Rolling Stones
nodes.summary: The British blues-rock band.

nodes.label: Michael Jackson
nodes.summary: The 'King of Pop'.

nodes.label: punk rock
nodes.summary: The stripped-down, fast, rebellious rock of the mid-1970s.

nodes.label: disco
nodes.summary: The dance-floor music of 1970s urban nightlife.

nodes.label: reggae
nodes.summary: The music that originated in Jamaica in the late 1960s.

nodes.label: Bob Marley
nodes.summary: The Jamaican who made reggae a global voice.

nodes.label: bossa nova
nodes.summary: The Brazilian fusion of samba and cool jazz.

nodes.label: Antônio Carlos Jobim
nodes.summary: The Brazilian composer of bossa nova.

nodes.label: Fela Kuti
nodes.summary: The Nigerian who created Afrobeat.

nodes.label: George Gershwin
nodes.summary: American composer who brought jazz into the concert hall.

nodes.label: salsa music
nodes.summary: The Afro-Cuban-rooted dance music of the Latin diaspora.

nodes.label: James Brown
nodes.summary: The 'Godfather of Soul', inventor of funk.

nodes.label: Albert Einstein
nodes.summary: The physicist of relativity.

nodes.label: theory of relativity
nodes.summary: Einstein's theories of special and general relativity.

nodes.label: quantum mechanics
nodes.summary: The physics of the atomic and subatomic world.

nodes.label: Manhattan Project
nodes.summary: The WWII US program that built the first atomic bombs.

nodes.label: nuclear weapon
nodes.summary: The atomic weapon.

nodes.label: V-2 rocket
nodes.summary: The Nazi German ballistic missile, ancestor of the space rocket.

nodes.label: Wernher von Braun
nodes.summary: The rocket engineer behind the V-2 and the Saturn V.

nodes.label: integrated circuit
nodes.summary: The microchip.

nodes.label: Silicon Valley
nodes.summary: The California heartland of the computer industry.

nodes.label: personal computer
nodes.summary: The computer for one person.

nodes.label: World Wide Web
nodes.summary: The web of hyperlinked pages atop the Internet.

nodes.label: Tim Berners-Lee
nodes.summary: The inventor of the World Wide Web.

nodes.label: John von Neumann
nodes.summary: The mathematician of the stored-program computer architecture.

nodes.label: Steve Jobs
nodes.summary: Co-founder of Apple; popularizer of the personal computer and smartphone.

nodes.label: Vladimir Lenin
nodes.summary: Leader of the Russian Revolution, founder of the USSR.

nodes.label: Russian Revolution
nodes.summary: The 1917 revolution that created the Soviet state.

nodes.label: Joseph Stalin
nodes.summary: The dictator of the Soviet Union.

nodes.label: Nikita Khrushchev
nodes.summary: Soviet leader of the space-race and Cuban-crisis years.

nodes.label: Berlin Wall
nodes.summary: The wall that sealed off East Berlin, 1961–1989.

nodes.label: Mao Zedong
nodes.summary: Founder of the People's Republic of China.

nodes.label: Chinese Communist Revolution
nodes.summary: The revolution that brought Mao's Communists to power in 1949.

nodes.label: Fidel Castro
nodes.summary: Leader of the Cuban Revolution and communist Cuba.

nodes.label: Che Guevara
nodes.summary: The Argentine-born icon of the Cuban Revolution.

nodes.label: Cuban Revolution
nodes.summary: The 1959 revolution that brought Castro to power.

nodes.label: Nelson Mandela
nodes.summary: Anti-apartheid leader and first Black president of South Africa.

nodes.label: Kwame Nkrumah
nodes.summary: Ghana's independence leader, a father of pan-Africanism.

nodes.label: Jawaharlal Nehru
nodes.summary: India's first prime minister, Gandhi's political heir.

nodes.label: John F. Kennedy
nodes.summary: US president who set the goal of landing on the Moon.

nodes.label: Beat Generation
nodes.summary: The 1950s American literary movement (Kerouac, Ginsberg).

statements.subject: star_wars
statements.evidence: Star Wars (1977) was written and directed by George Lucas.
statements.headline: Star Wars was written and directed by George Lucas.

statements.subject: star_wars
statements.evidence: George Lucas has repeatedly cited Kurosawa's The Hidden Fortress as an influence on Star Wars, including telling the story through two low-status bickering characters.
statements.headline: George Lucas modelled Star Wars partly on Kurosawa's samurai film The Hidden Fortress.

statements.subject: the_hidden_fortress
statements.evidence: The Hidden Fortress (1958) was directed and co-written by Akira Kurosawa.
statements.headline: The Hidden Fortress is a 1958 samurai adventure directed by Akira Kurosawa.

statements.subject: george_lucas
statements.evidence: George Lucas credited Joseph Campbell's monomyth, the 'hero's journey', as a framework for Star Wars and called Campbell a mentor.
statements.headline: George Lucas built Star Wars on Joseph Campbell's 'hero's journey' monomyth.

statements.subject: star_wars
statements.evidence: Star Wars (1977) pioneered computer-controlled motion-control photography, and Lucas's ILM later drove the rise of digital visual effects.
statements.headline: Star Wars pioneered computer-controlled cameras and helped launch digital visual effects.

statements.subject: computer
statements.evidence: The modern stored-program computer realises Alan Turing's 1936 concept of a 'universal machine'.
statements.headline: Every modern computer is a physical realisation of Alan Turing's 1936 'universal machine'.

statements.subject: transistor
statements.evidence: The transistor replaced the vacuum tube as the fundamental switching element of the modern computer.
statements.headline: The transistor is the building block every modern computer is made of.

statements.subject: jazz
statements.evidence: Jazz grew out of the blues and ragtime in early-20th-century African-American communities.
statements.headline: Jazz grew out of the blues in the African-American South.

statements.subject: rock_and_roll
statements.evidence: Rock and roll developed in the 1950s out of rhythm and blues, itself a descendant of the blues.
statements.headline: Rock and roll grew directly out of African-American rhythm and blues.

statements.subject: the_beatles
statements.evidence: The Beatles began as a rock and roll band, covering 1950s American rock and roll before writing their own songs.
statements.headline: The Beatles started out as a 1950s rock and roll cover band.

statements.subject: the_beatles
statements.evidence: George Harrison studied the sitar under Ravi Shankar, bringing Indian classical music into the Beatles' later work.
statements.headline: The Beatles' Indian-music phase came from George Harrison studying sitar under Ravi Shankar.

statements.subject: ravi_shankar
statements.evidence: Ravi Shankar was a master of Hindustani classical music, the North Indian art-music tradition.
statements.headline: Ravi Shankar was a grandmaster of the North Indian classical sitar.

statements.subject: rock_and_roll
statements.evidence: The cheap transistor radio of the 1950s spread rock and roll to a mass teenage audience, turning it into a youth phenomenon.
statements.headline: The transistor radio carried rock and roll into every 1950s teenager's pocket.

statements.subject: civil_rights_movement
statements.evidence: Jazz and its musicians were woven into the American civil-rights movement as an assertion of Black cultural pride, from Nina Simone's protest songs to benefit concerts.
statements.headline: Jazz became a musical voice of the American civil-rights movement.

statements.subject: martin_luther_king
statements.evidence: Martin Luther King Jr. was the most prominent leader of the American civil-rights movement.
statements.headline: Martin Luther King Jr. led the American civil-rights movement.

statements.subject: martin_luther_king
statements.evidence: Martin Luther King Jr. adopted Gandhi's method of non-violent civil disobedience as the core strategy of the American civil-rights movement.
statements.headline: Martin Luther King Jr. took his non-violent strategy from Mahatma Gandhi.

statements.subject: seven_samurai
statements.evidence: Seven Samurai (1954) was directed and co-written by Akira Kurosawa.
statements.headline: Seven Samurai is Akira Kurosawa's 1954 masterpiece.

statements.subject: rashomon
statements.evidence: Rashomon (1950) was directed by Akira Kurosawa and gave English the phrase 'the Rashomon effect'.
statements.headline: Kurosawa's Rashomon gave English the phrase 'the Rashomon effect'.

statements.subject: the_magnificent_seven
statements.evidence: The Magnificent Seven (1960) is a Hollywood Western re-telling of Kurosawa's Seven Samurai.
statements.headline: The Magnificent Seven is a Hollywood Western remake of Kurosawa's Seven Samurai.

statements.subject: civil_rights_movement
statements.evidence: The freedom songs that carried the civil-rights movement were largely adapted from Black gospel hymns.
statements.headline: The civil-rights movement's freedom songs came out of Black gospel music.

statements.subject: soul_music
statements.evidence: Soul music grew directly out of Black gospel music in the late 1950s, keeping its vocal fervour.
statements.headline: Soul music grew directly out of Black gospel music.

statements.subject: the_beatles
statements.evidence: The Beatles' early repertoire drew heavily on American soul and Motown records.
statements.headline: The Beatles' early sound leaned on American soul and Motown records.

statements.subject: funk
statements.evidence: Funk emerged in the mid-1960s out of soul, jazz and R&B, built on syncopated basslines and grooves.
statements.headline: Funk emerged out of soul, jazz and R&B in the mid-1960s.

statements.subject: hip_hop
statements.evidence: Hip hop was built in the 1970s Bronx from funk and soul breakbeats looped by DJs.
statements.headline: Hip hop was built from funk and soul breakbeats in the 1970s Bronx.

statements.subject: the_beatles
statements.evidence: From 1964 Bob Dylan pushed the Beatles toward introspective, literary lyrics.
statements.headline: Bob Dylan pushed the Beatles toward introspective, literary songwriting.

statements.subject: the_internet
statements.evidence: The Internet is a global network of interconnected computers, grown from 1960s packet-switching research.
statements.headline: The Internet is a global network built by wiring computers together.

statements.subject: television
statements.evidence: The transistor made the television set compact, low-power and cheap enough for every home.
statements.headline: The transistor turned television into a compact mass-market appliance.

statements.subject: yuri_gagarin
statements.evidence: Four years after Sputnik, the USSR again led the Space Race by putting Yuri Gagarin into orbit in 1961.
statements.headline: Yuri Gagarin became the first human in space, extending the USSR's Sputnik lead.

statements.subject: apollo_11
statements.evidence: The shock of the Soviet Sputnik launch spurred the US crash programme that culminated in the Apollo 11 Moon landing.
statements.headline: America raced to the Moon because the Soviet Sputnik got there into orbit first.

statements.subject: apollo_11
statements.evidence: The Apollo Guidance Computer, one of the first integrated-circuit computers, navigated Apollo 11 to the Moon.
statements.headline: Apollo 11 reached the Moon steered by one of the first integrated-circuit computers.

statements.subject: tetris
statements.evidence: Tetris was written for a Soviet computer in 1984, then spread worldwide as a defining early video game.
statements.headline: Tetris, a global gaming phenomenon, was written on a computer in the Soviet Union.

statements.subject: elvis_presley
statements.evidence: Elvis Presley was the breakout star who carried rock and roll into the American mainstream.
statements.headline: Elvis Presley was the breakout star of early rock and roll.

statements.subject: the_beatles
statements.evidence: The Beatles idolised Elvis Presley, whose rock and roll set them on their path.
statements.headline: The Beatles idolised Elvis Presley, whose records set them on their path.

statements.subject: international_style
statements.evidence: Modernism's stripped International Style succeeded the decorative Art Deco of the 1920s–30s.
statements.headline: The plain International Style succeeded the ornament of Art Deco.

statements.subject: bauhaus
statements.evidence: The Bauhaus drew on the geometric abstraction and social ambition of Soviet Constructivism.
statements.headline: The German Bauhaus drew on the Soviet Constructivist avant-garde.

statements.subject: mies_van_der_rohe
statements.evidence: Ludwig Mies van der Rohe was the last director of the Bauhaus before it closed.
statements.headline: Mies van der Rohe was the Bauhaus's final director.

statements.subject: international_style
statements.evidence: The International Style grew directly out of the Bauhaus's functionalist teaching.
statements.headline: The International Style grew out of the Bauhaus.

statements.subject: le_corbusier
statements.evidence: Le Corbusier was a defining figure of the International Style.
statements.headline: Le Corbusier was a defining figure of the International Style.

statements.subject: international_style
statements.evidence: Frank Lloyd Wright's open plans and cantilevers influenced the European modernists.
statements.headline: Frank Lloyd Wright's work influenced European modern architecture.

statements.subject: brutalism
statements.evidence: Brutalism grew out of Le Corbusier's raw-concrete (beton brut) buildings.
statements.headline: Brutalism grew out of Le Corbusier's raw-concrete work.

statements.subject: chandigarh
statements.evidence: The Indian city of Chandigarh was master-planned by Le Corbusier.
statements.headline: The Indian city of Chandigarh was designed by Le Corbusier.

statements.subject: sydney_opera_house
statements.evidence: The Sydney Opera House pushed modernist architecture into expressive, sculptural form.
statements.headline: The Sydney Opera House carried modern architecture into sculptural form.

statements.subject: hollywood
statements.evidence: Art Deco defined the glamour of Hollywood's golden age, from its cinemas to its set design.
statements.headline: Art Deco set the glamorous look of golden-age Hollywood.

statements.subject: star_wars
statements.evidence: Star Wars was made and distributed by the Hollywood studio system.
statements.headline: Star Wars was a product of the Hollywood studio system.

statements.subject: the_magnificent_seven
statements.evidence: The Magnificent Seven was a Hollywood studio Western.
statements.headline: The Magnificent Seven was a Hollywood studio Western.

statements.subject: hollywood
statements.evidence: Kurosawa's films profoundly shaped Hollywood, from Westerns to Star Wars.
statements.headline: Akira Kurosawa's films profoundly shaped Hollywood.

statements.subject: walt_disney
statements.evidence: Walt Disney built his animation empire within Hollywood.
statements.headline: Walt Disney built his animation studio in Hollywood.

statements.subject: alfred_hitchcock
statements.evidence: Alfred Hitchcock made his most famous thrillers in Hollywood.
statements.headline: Alfred Hitchcock made his classic thrillers in Hollywood.

statements.subject: citizen_kane
statements.evidence: Citizen Kane was Orson Welles's Hollywood debut.
statements.headline: Citizen Kane was Orson Welles's Hollywood debut.

statements.subject: charlie_chaplin
statements.evidence: Charlie Chaplin became the biggest star of early Hollywood.
statements.headline: Charlie Chaplin was the biggest star of early Hollywood.

statements.subject: steven_spielberg
statements.evidence: Steven Spielberg defined the modern Hollywood blockbuster.
statements.headline: Steven Spielberg defined the modern Hollywood blockbuster.

statements.subject: stanley_kubrick
statements.evidence: Stanley Kubrick worked within, and against, the Hollywood system.
statements.headline: Stanley Kubrick worked within the Hollywood system.

statements.subject: sergio_leone
statements.evidence: Leone's A Fistful of Dollars was an unofficial remake of Kurosawa's Yojimbo.
statements.headline: Sergio Leone remade Kurosawa's Yojimbo as A Fistful of Dollars.

statements.subject: french_new_wave
statements.evidence: The New Wave critics idolised Hitchcock; Truffaut wrote the book on him.
statements.headline: The French New Wave idolised Alfred Hitchcock.

statements.subject: french_new_wave
statements.evidence: The New Wave built on neorealism's location shooting and small budgets.
statements.headline: The French New Wave built on Italian neorealism.

statements.subject: satyajit_ray
statements.evidence: Satyajit Ray credited De Sica's Bicycle Thieves as the spark for the Apu Trilogy.
statements.headline: Satyajit Ray's Indian cinema was sparked by Italian neorealism.

statements.subject: bollywood
statements.evidence: Bollywood adapted Hollywood's genres and star system to Indian melodrama and song.
statements.headline: Bollywood grew up adapting Hollywood's genres to Indian song and melodrama.

statements.subject: osamu_tezuka
statements.evidence: Tezuka, the 'god of manga', modelled his big-eyed style on Disney animation.
statements.headline: The father of anime, Osamu Tezuka, modelled his style on Walt Disney.

statements.subject: anime
statements.evidence: Tezuka's Astro Boy set the template for modern TV anime.
statements.headline: Modern anime follows the template Osamu Tezuka set with Astro Boy.

statements.subject: louis_armstrong
statements.evidence: Louis Armstrong turned jazz into a soloist's art.
statements.headline: Louis Armstrong made jazz a soloist's art.

statements.subject: miles_davis
statements.evidence: Miles Davis reinvented jazz repeatedly, from bebop to fusion.
statements.headline: Miles Davis reinvented jazz from bebop to fusion.

statements.subject: chuck_berry
statements.evidence: Chuck Berry's guitar and songwriting defined rock and roll.
statements.headline: Chuck Berry's guitar defined rock and roll.

statements.subject: the_beatles
statements.evidence: The Beatles cut their teeth covering Chuck Berry.
statements.headline: The Beatles cut their teeth on Chuck Berry covers.

statements.subject: the_rolling_stones
statements.evidence: The Rolling Stones began as a Chicago-blues covers band.
statements.headline: The Rolling Stones began as a Chicago-blues covers band.

statements.subject: james_brown
statements.evidence: James Brown, the 'Godfather of Soul', invented funk.
statements.headline: James Brown invented funk.

statements.subject: michael_jackson
statements.evidence: Michael Jackson modelled his dancing and showmanship on James Brown.
statements.headline: Michael Jackson modelled his moves on James Brown.

statements.subject: punk_rock
statements.evidence: Punk stripped rock and roll back to fast, loud basics.
statements.headline: Punk stripped rock and roll back to fast, loud basics.

statements.subject: punk_rock
statements.evidence: British punk absorbed Jamaican reggae; The Clash covered it outright.
statements.headline: British punk soaked up Jamaican reggae.

statements.subject: disco
statements.evidence: Disco grew out of funk and soul for the dance floor.
statements.headline: Disco grew out of funk for the dance floor.

statements.subject: reggae
statements.evidence: Jamaican reggae grew partly out of imported American R&B and soul.
statements.headline: Jamaican reggae grew partly out of American soul and R&B.

statements.subject: bob_marley
statements.evidence: Bob Marley made reggae a global music.
statements.headline: Bob Marley made reggae a global music.

statements.subject: bossa_nova
statements.evidence: Bossa nova fused Brazilian samba with American cool jazz.
statements.headline: Bossa nova fused Brazilian samba with American cool jazz.

statements.subject: antonio_carlos_jobim
statements.evidence: Antônio Carlos Jobim composed the songs that defined bossa nova.
statements.headline: Antônio Carlos Jobim composed the songs that defined bossa nova.

statements.subject: fela_kuti
statements.evidence: Fela Kuti fused Yoruba music, jazz and James Brown's funk into Afrobeat.
statements.headline: Fela Kuti fused Yoruba music with American funk to make Afrobeat.

statements.subject: george_gershwin
statements.evidence: Gershwin brought jazz idioms into the concert hall with Rhapsody in Blue.
statements.headline: George Gershwin carried jazz into the concert hall.

statements.subject: salsa_music
statements.evidence: Salsa fused Afro-Cuban son with the horns and improvisation of jazz.
statements.headline: Salsa fused Afro-Cuban son with jazz.

statements.subject: theory_of_relativity
statements.evidence: The theory of relativity was Albert Einstein's work.
statements.headline: The theory of relativity was Albert Einstein's.

statements.subject: quantum_mechanics
statements.evidence: Einstein's explanation of the photoelectric effect helped found quantum theory.
statements.headline: Einstein's photoelectric-effect paper helped found quantum mechanics.

statements.subject: transistor
statements.evidence: The transistor works by the quantum behaviour of electrons in semiconductors.
statements.headline: The transistor runs on the quantum physics of semiconductors.

statements.subject: manhattan_project
statements.evidence: Einstein's 1939 letter to Roosevelt set the Manhattan Project in motion.
statements.headline: Einstein's letter to Roosevelt launched the Manhattan Project.

statements.subject: nuclear_weapon
statements.evidence: The first nuclear weapons were built by the Manhattan Project.
statements.headline: The first nuclear weapons came out of the Manhattan Project.

statements.subject: nuclear_weapon
statements.evidence: The bomb's energy is the mass-energy equivalence, E=mc², of relativity.
statements.headline: The atomic bomb's power is Einstein's E=mc² made real.

statements.subject: v2_rocket
statements.evidence: The V-2, the first long-range guided ballistic missile, was designed by von Braun.
statements.headline: The V-2 rocket was designed by Wernher von Braun.

statements.subject: apollo_11
statements.evidence: The Saturn V that launched Apollo 11 descended from von Braun's V-2.
statements.headline: Apollo 11's rocket descended from the Nazi V-2.

statements.subject: apollo_11
statements.evidence: Von Braun led the design of the Saturn V that carried Apollo 11 to the Moon.
statements.headline: Wernher von Braun built the rocket that took Apollo 11 to the Moon.

statements.subject: integrated_circuit
statements.evidence: The integrated circuit packs many transistors onto one chip.
statements.headline: The microchip packs many transistors onto one sliver of silicon.

statements.subject: personal_computer
statements.evidence: The personal computer became possible once integrated circuits were cheap.
statements.headline: The personal computer was born when microchips got cheap.

statements.subject: personal_computer
statements.evidence: The personal computer was largely created by Silicon Valley firms.
statements.headline: The personal computer was largely a Silicon Valley creation.

statements.subject: steve_jobs
statements.evidence: Steve Jobs co-founded Apple in Silicon Valley.
statements.headline: Steve Jobs co-founded Apple in Silicon Valley.

statements.subject: personal_computer
statements.evidence: Steve Jobs's Apple made the personal computer a mass-market product.
statements.headline: Steve Jobs made the personal computer a mass-market product.

statements.subject: world_wide_web
statements.evidence: The World Wide Web is a service running on the Internet.
statements.headline: The World Wide Web is a service running on the Internet.

statements.subject: world_wide_web
statements.evidence: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN in 1989.
statements.headline: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web at CERN.

statements.subject: computer
statements.evidence: The stored-program design of nearly all computers is the von Neumann architecture.
statements.headline: Nearly every computer follows John von Neumann's stored-program design.

statements.subject: russian_revolution
statements.evidence: Lenin's Bolsheviks led the 1917 Russian Revolution.
statements.headline: Lenin's Bolsheviks led the Russian Revolution.

statements.subject: joseph_stalin
statements.evidence: Stalin succeeded Lenin as leader of the Soviet Union.
statements.headline: Stalin succeeded Lenin atop the Soviet Union.

statements.subject: nikita_khrushchev
statements.evidence: Khrushchev took power after Stalin's death and denounced him.
statements.headline: Khrushchev took over after Stalin and denounced him.

statements.subject: berlin_wall
statements.evidence: The Berlin Wall was built in 1961 under Khrushchev to seal off East Berlin.
statements.headline: The Berlin Wall went up in 1961 under Khrushchev.

statements.subject: sputnik
statements.evidence: Khrushchev seized on Sputnik as proof of Soviet superiority.
statements.headline: Khrushchev turned Sputnik into a Cold War trophy.

statements.subject: chinese_communist_revolution
statements.evidence: Mao led the Communists to victory in the Chinese Civil War.
statements.headline: Mao Zedong led the Chinese Communist Revolution to victory.

statements.subject: chinese_communist_revolution
statements.evidence: China's revolution followed the Marxist-Leninist model of the Russian Revolution.
statements.headline: China's revolution followed the Soviet Marxist-Leninist model.

statements.subject: cuban_revolution
statements.evidence: Fidel Castro led the Cuban Revolution to power in 1959.
statements.headline: Fidel Castro led the Cuban Revolution to power.

statements.subject: cuban_revolution
statements.evidence: Che Guevara was a central commander of the Cuban Revolution.
statements.headline: Che Guevara was a commander of the Cuban Revolution.

statements.subject: cuban_revolution
statements.evidence: Castro's Cuba adopted the Soviet Marxist-Leninist model and allied with the USSR.
statements.headline: Revolutionary Cuba took up the Soviet model and allied with Moscow.

statements.subject: nelson_mandela
statements.evidence: Mandela drew on Gandhi's methods, which Gandhi first developed in South Africa.
statements.headline: Nelson Mandela drew on Gandhi, who forged his methods in South Africa.

statements.subject: kwame_nkrumah
statements.evidence: Nkrumah's Ghana independence campaign used Gandhian 'positive action'.
statements.headline: Kwame Nkrumah freed Ghana with Gandhian non-violence.

statements.subject: jawaharlal_nehru
statements.evidence: Nehru, India's first prime minister, was Gandhi's chosen political heir.
statements.headline: Nehru, free India's first PM, was Gandhi's political heir.

statements.subject: apollo_11
statements.evidence: Kennedy's 1961 pledge to land a man on the Moon set Apollo 11 in motion.
statements.headline: Apollo 11 fulfilled John F. Kennedy's pledge to reach the Moon.

statements.subject: beat_generation
statements.evidence: The Beat writers took their spontaneous rhythm from bebop jazz.
statements.headline: The Beat writers took their rhythm from bebop jazz.

statements.subject: bob_dylan
statements.evidence: Dylan's free-verse lyrics were shaped by the Beat poets.
statements.headline: Bob Dylan's lyrics were shaped by the Beat poets.
